evidence: All 77 milestone_record tests pass (0 failures, 0 ignored)
cargo clippy -- -D warnings passes cleanly (no warnings)
cargo fmt --all --check passes (no formatting issues)
service.rs: `record_task_run_start` (line 97-106) is now a required trait method with no default implementation — addresses amendment fr-6-93044933
service.rs: `append_event_if_missing` (line 74-79) is now a required trait method with no default implementation — addresses amendment fr-6-81c4543f
service.rs: `MilestoneSnapshotPort::with_milestone_write_lock` (line 50-58) added as a required trait method, returning `AppResult<T>` from a closure run under the lock
fs.rs: `FsMilestoneSnapshotStore::with_milestone_write_lock` (line 2254-2265) acquires an `AdvisoryFileLock` on `mutation.lock` before executing the closure, serializing all milestone mutations

service.rs: `record_bead_start` (line 420) wraps its entire body in `snapshot_store.with_milestone_write_lock()`, including lineage write, snapshot reconcile, and journal append — addresses amendments fr-6-459caba1 and fr-6-ff09cb64

service.rs: `update_task_run` (line 552) wraps its entire body in `snapshot_store.with_milestone_write_lock()`, including lineage update, snapshot reconcile, and journal append — addresses amendments fr-6-459caba1 and fr-6-ff09cb64

service.rs: `record_bead_start` checks `snapshot.status.is_terminal()` at lines 427-434 BEFORE calling `record_task_run_start` at line 436, preventing stray lineage writes on terminal milestones — addresses amendment fr-6-0ee39bfb
service.rs: `update_status` (line 329) and `persist_plan` (line 372) also run under `with_milestone_write_lock`, ensuring all milestone mutation paths are serialized

Test: `terminal_milestone_rejects_bead_start_without_mutating_lineage` (line 1017) verifies: error contains 'already completed', snapshot stays Completed, runs are empty, no BeadStarted journal event — validates fr-6-0ee39bfb fix

Test: `start_and_completion_serialize_milestone_snapshot_updates` (line 2270) uses a BlockingSnapshotStore to verify: new bead start blocks until in-flight completion releases the write lock; final snapshot has correct active_bead='bead-2', completed_beads=1, in_progress_beads=1 — validates fr-6-ff09cb64 fix
Test: `concurrent_start_retries_share_one_lineage_row_and_start_event` (line 2072+) verifies two concurrent identical starts produce exactly 1 lineage row and 1 BeadStarted journal event

fs.rs: `FsTaskRunLineageStore::record_task_run_start` (line 2526-2603) still acquires its own advisory lock on `task-runs.lock` for the read-check-append/reuse sequence, providing defense-in-depth alongside the milestone mutation lock
fs.rs: `FsMilestoneJournalStore::append_event_if_missing` (line 2306-2327) still acquires its own advisory lock on journal.lock for atomic deduplicated appends

Test mock `FailSecondJournalAppend` (line 636-651) and `FailFirstJournalAppend` (line 681-695) provide their own `append_event_if_missing` implementations since the trait no longer provides a default — these are test-only (#[cfg(test)]) and single-threaded, so non-atomic implementation is acceptable

model.rs: TaskRunEntry has all required fields — milestone_id, bead_id, project_id, run_id, plan_hash, outcome, outcome_detail, started_at, finished_at — with serde(default, skip_serializing_if) for backward compatibility
model.rs: MilestoneProgress.skipped_beads with serde(default), remaining() accounts for it
service.rs: reconcile_snapshot_from_lineage (line 163-219) rebuilds all counters from canonical lineage, correctly transitions Active→Ready when no active beads remain
findings_or_gaps: All five final-review amendments (fr-6-93044933, fr-6-81c4543f, fr-6-459caba1, fr-6-0ee39bfb, fr-6-ff09cb64) have been addressed with code changes and regression tests in this round.

The milestone mutation lock is milestone-scoped (one lock file per milestone at mutation.lock), so mutations to different milestones can still proceed concurrently — this is correct granularity for the use case.

The lineage-level locks (task-runs.lock, journal.lock) are retained alongside the new milestone mutation lock, providing defense-in-depth. The nesting order is: milestone mutation lock (outer) → lineage/journal lock (inner). Since both use advisory file locks with flock(), and the inner lock is always acquired after the outer lock within the same call chain, there is no deadlock risk.

AppError::RunStartFailed is still used (service.rs line 554) for rejecting non-terminal outcomes in update_task_run, which is semantically a finalization rejection. A more specific error variant would be cleaner but this is cosmetic, not a correctness issue — carried forward from prior reviews.

The outstanding risk — that milestone mutations still span separate lineage, snapshot, and journal files without a cross-file transaction — remains. However, all paths are now (a) serialized by the milestone mutation lock so concurrent writers cannot interleave, (b) replay-safe so a crash between writes can be repaired by retrying. This is an acceptable trade-off for file-based storage.

The BlockingSnapshotStore test mock (line 698-750) delegates `with_milestone_write_lock` to the real `FsMilestoneSnapshotStore` implementation (line 748), so the serialization test exercises the actual file-based lock — not a test-only approximation.
